claims.summary: Tail vibrancy may be mistaken for retained semantic distinguishability in the 48D lane.
claims.disposition: Verified the codec records tail-bridge activation separately from projection health, resonance loss, and distinguishability evidence.

claims.summary: Projection and packing health need evidence beyond visible tail motion.
claims.disposition: Verified deterministic projection-basis health and bounded pairwise distinguishability diagnostics already expose that distinction.

claims.summary: High-entropy and mode-sensitive inputs should be compared across the packing path.
claims.disposition: Routed to an offline fixture comparing entropy, mode packing, tail activation, and distinguishability without transport changes.

claims.summary: Feature clamps, gate shape, or dynamically reserved dimensions could preserve more signal.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because these changes alter the live codec transform and Minime-facing sensory contract.
